description:
# Why

bl-ffb4's master_url path bypasses setup_state_branch entirely, and that function is where ensure_tasks_symlink lives — so a master_url-configured repo never gets the convenience symlink .balls/tasks -> .balls/state-repo/.balls/tasks (the legacy mode points it at worktree/.balls/tasks).

bl itself works because Store routes through tasks_dir_path (which resolves correctly to .balls/state-repo/.balls/tasks); the regression is purely the stock-tool ergonomic the README advertises:

> An engineer on main can `ls .balls/tasks/`, `cat`, and `$EDITOR` files through this symlink without any balls-specific knowledge.

# Concrete fix

state_repo::ensure() should create the symlink alongside seed() — same shape as ensure_tasks_symlink in store_init.rs but with the target swapped to state-repo/.balls/tasks. Keep the bl-init refusal-on-non-symlink guard.

# Out of scope

The two layouts are mutually exclusive (master_url wins), so there's no "both symlinks" case to worry about. The legacy mode keeps using setup_state_branch's existing symlink.
title: .balls/tasks symlink not created in master_url mode
